intended_role: VP Sales leading a regional enterprise sales team

organization.role_title: VP Sales leading a regional enterprise sales team

contextual_signals.best_role_ever: Revenue culture with clear targets, fast feedback, and room to coach in the field.
contextual_signals.worst_role_ever: Over-processed sales environment where every move is mediated through internal approvals.
contextual_signals.current_energy_drain: Slow handoffs, excessive documentation, and non-commercial ambiguity.
contextual_signals.recurring_org_frustration: May over-trust momentum and relationship heat before qualification is fully verified.
contextual_signals.relied_on_for: Turns human dynamics into forward commercial motion.
contextual_signals.misunderstood_for: May over-trust momentum and relationship heat before qualification is fully verified.
contextual_signals.unrealized_capacity: Strong VP Sales fit, with pipeline inspection and deal-quality discipline needed.

q2.text: I want my work to create freedom for my family and a team that knows winning can be disciplined, not frantic. I care about income, reputation, and proving that relationships can become real business value. In ten years I want a sales organization that grows leaders, not just quota performers.

q14.text: A large prospect went quiet after a champion left the company. I called the new buyer too quickly and realized later I had not rebuilt trust first. I slowed down, mapped the stakeholders, and rebuilt the opportunity through a different entry point.

q17.text: Under pressure I get direct, energetic, and very focused on the next move. People feel my urgency. The downside is I can make slower teammates feel like they are dragging the whole room.

q20.text: I once advanced a deal stage with incomplete technical confirmation because the buyer energy was strong. It created a scramble later. I learned to separate buyer enthusiasm from deal truth.

q22.text: I would rate myself a 7.5. I can motivate and redirect people quickly, but I still have to work on inspection discipline so coaching does not depend too much on instinct.

q24.text: When momentum stalls I look for the person with influence and try to change the conversation. I get frustrated by vague ownership and weak follow-up. I avoid sitting with quiet pipeline risk longer than I should because I prefer action.

q25.text: I usually get on the phone and reset the conversation. I want to hear the concern, but I also want to keep the relationship moving.

q26.text: I naturally become the closer, coach, and pressure carrier. Tension appears when operations needs more precision than my field read initially provides.

q27.text: I am trying to build a sales culture where people can win without becoming transactional. I value courage, consistency, and relational leverage.

q28.text: Forecast calls, deal reviews, and one-on-ones keep work organized. The scaling strain will be quality control: making sure the team does not confuse activity with real progression.
